sourceMovie.title: 100 METERS
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit 100 METERS for the same reason it broke through in 2025: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Kenji Iwaisawa keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The First Slam Dunk
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 100 METERS clicked for you, The First Slam Dunk (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 100 METERS clicked for you, The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after 100 METERS?
faq.answer: Try The First Slam Dunk, The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes, The Prestige—each shares DNA with 100 METERS while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 100 METERS?
faq.answer: 100 METERS (2025) is best described as Animation, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 100 METERS worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
